channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Amanda Knox gets a final shot at clearing her name of slander in Italy's top court
item.description: ROME (AP) — Amanda Knox has a final shot at clearing her name of the last vestige of criminal wrongdoing when Italy’s highest court on Thursday hears her appeal of a slander conviction for falsely accusing a Congolese bar owner in the 2007 murder of her British flatmate.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD and COLLEEN BARRY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Trump heads back to Davos, this time virtually, for elite World Economic Forum gathering
item.description: U.S. President Trump is to speak to an international audience for the first time after returning into the White House with a speech and Q&A by video conference to the World Economic Forum’s annual event in Davos on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Jamey Keaten, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Families sue TikTok in France over teen suicides they say are linked to harmful content
item.description: Seven families are suing TikTok in France, accusing the platform of failing to moderate harmful content and exposing children to life-threatening material.
item.dc:creator: Tom Nouvian, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Teen who killed 3 girls at Taylor Swift-themed dance class in England faces sentencing
item.description: A violence-obsessed teenager is due to be sentenced for stabbing three young girls to death at a Taylor Swift-themed summer dance class.
item.dc:creator: Jill Lawless, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: LGBTQ+ couples in Thailand register their marriages as law gives them equal status
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Hundreds of LGBTQ+ couples in Thailand are expected to make their wedded status legal Thursday, the day a law took effect granting them the same rights as heterosexual couples.
item.dc:creator: CHALIDA EKVITTHAYAVECHNUKUL, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: South Korean investigators call for indictment of detained President Yoon
item.description: South Korean investigators asked prosecutors to indict the country’s detained President Yoon Suk Yeol over his short-lived imposition of martial law last month, as it accused him of rebellion, abuse of power and obstruction of parliament.
item.dc:creator: Hyung-Jin Kim, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump talks tough with Putin on ending Ukraine war
item.description: President Trump is turning up the heat on Russian President Vladimir Putin in his first days in power, seeking to pressure Russia’s leader to come to the negotiating table for a deal to end the war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Mexican border states prepare migrant shelters as Trump begins deportation campaign
item.description: Mexico was raising sprawling tents on the U.S. border Wednesday as it braced for President Donald Trump to fulfill his pledge to reverse mass migration.
item.dc:creator: Martín Silva Rey And Félix Márquez, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Italy under fire from International Criminal Court for releasing Libya warlord accused of war crimes
item.description: ROME (AP) — Human rights groups voiced outrage Wednesday after Italy released a Libyan warlord on a technicality, after he was arrested on a warrant from the International Criminal Court accusing him of war crimes and crimes against humanity.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD and SAM MAGDY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Middle East latest: Israel says it killed a militant in Gaza as fragile ceasefire holds
item.description: The Israeli military said Wednesday its soldiers killed an alleged militant in Gaza whom it said posed a threat to its forces, as the ceasefire between Israel and Hamas stretched into a fourth day.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Murdoch's UK tabloids apologize to Prince Harry and admit intruding on the late Princess Diana
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Prince Harry claimed a “monumental” victory Wednesday as Rupert Murdoch’s U.K. tabloids made an unprecedented apology for intruding in his life for years, and agreed to pay substantial damages to settle his privacy invasion lawsuit.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN MELLEY and JILL LAWLESS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Afghans who fled Taliban rule urge Trump to lift refugee program suspension
item.description: ISLAMABAD (AP) — Afghans who fled after the Taliban seized power appealed Wednesday to U.S. President Donald Trump to exempt them from an order suspending the relocation of refugees to the United States, some saying they risked their lives to support U.S. troops.
item.dc:creator: MUNIR AHMED, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Taiwan Air Force officer killed after being ‘inhaled’ by fighter jet’s engine
item.description: A Taiwan Air Force officer died after being sucked into the engine of an Indigenous Defense Fighter jet at Ching Chuan Kang Air Base, officials say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A rebel group is advancing on eastern Congo's largest city and over 100,000 people have fled
item.description: NZULO, Congo (AP) — The M23 rebel group's advance toward eastern Congo's largest city has displaced over 178,000 people in the past two weeks, the United Nations said, as the fighters closed in on Goma on the border with Rwanda.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN KABUMBA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump threatens Russia with taxes, tariffs, sanctions if 'ridiculous' Ukraine war not ended
item.description: President Trump on Wednesday threatened to impose tariffs and sanctions on Russia if there is no deal to stop the fighting in Ukraine "soon." "I’m not looking to hurt Russia.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: NFL legends make emotional trip to Israel in push for hostages' release
item.description: Former NFL stars Nick Lowery and Tony Richardson traveled to Israel where they met with leaders and the family of Hamas hostage Naama Levy in an effort to bring attention to the plight of the captives.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: At least 76 people killed in a hotel fire at a ski resort in Turkey
item.description: ANKARA, Turkey (AP) — A fire raged through a 12-story hotel at a popular ski resort in northwestern Turkey early Tuesday during a school holiday, killing at least 76 people — at least two of them when they jumped from the building to escape the flames, officials said.
item.dc:creator: SUZAN FRASER, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: At least 80 people killed in northeast Colombia as peace talks fail, official says
item.description: More than 80 people were killed in northeast Colombia over the weekend after the government failed to hold peace talks with the National Liberation Army.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The rescue of hundreds of miners in a deep South African gold mine began with a camera and a note
item.description: CARLETONVILLE, South Africa (AP) — A specialized camera was lowered late last year into an almost 2.6-kilometer- (1.6-mile-) deep mineshaft in South Africa where hundreds of miners were reported to be trapped, starving, dehydrated and desperate to get out.
item.dc:creator: MOGOMOTSI MAGOME, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump: Russia sanctions ‘likely’ if Moscow doesn’t negotiate Ukraine ceasefire
item.description: President Trump said Tuesday it was likely he would impose additional sanctions on Russia if it did not come to the negotiating table to broker a ceasefire in Ukraine. "Sounds likely," Trump said of potential sanctions on Moscow.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Israel's top general resigns over Oct. 7 failures, adding to pressure on Netanyahu
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israel's top general resigned Tuesday, taking responsibility for security failures tied to Hamas' surprise attack that triggered the war in Gaza and adding to pressure on Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu, who has delayed any public inquiry that could potentially implicate his leade
item.dc:creator: MELANIE LIDMAN and AREF TUFAHA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Stefanik vows to keep the U.S. engaged at U.N. to counter China
item.description: Rep. Elise Stefanik (R-N.Y.) assured skeptical Senate Democrats during her confirmation hearing as President Trump's pick for U.N. ambassador that she is committed to engaging in the United Nations, saying it’s a priority venue to challenge China’s push for dominance on the global stage.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Colombia's president will declare an emergency over deadly ELN attacks in the northeast
item.description: BOGOTA, Colombia (AP) — Colombian President Gustavo Petro said Monday that he will declare a state of emergency over the guerrilla attacks in the northeast that have killed dozens of people and forced thousands to flee their homes.
item.dc:creator: MANUEL RUEDA and ASTRID SUÁREZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia warns Trump against seizing Panama Canal
item.description: Russian officials reaffirmed their commitment to the neutrality of the critical trade route and said it expects both America and Panama to continue respecting that agreement.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: Trump announces second withdrawal from Paris Climate Agreement
item.description: In his first term, Trump announced the pullout in 2017, with the process becoming official two years later.
item.dc:creator: Zack Budryk, NewsNation

item.title: Pope Francis hurts his right arm after falling for the second time in just over a month
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis fell Thursday and hurt his right arm, the Vatican said, just weeks after another apparent fall resulted in a bad bruise on his chin. Francis didn’t break his arm, but a sling was put on as a precaution, the Vatican spokesman said in a statement On Dec.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Netanyahu says 'last-minute crisis' with Hamas holding up approval of Gaza truce and hostage deal
item.description: TEL AVIV, Israel (AP) — Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu’s office said Thursday his Cabinet won’t meet to approve the agreement for a ceasefire in the Gaza Strip and the release of dozens of hostages until Hamas backs down, accusing the group of reneging on parts of the agreement in an atte
item.dc:creator: TIA GOLDENBERG, WAFAA SHURAFA and SAMY MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Two Americans to be released from Gaza in first phase of ceasefire deal
item.description: Two Americans, Keith Siegel and Sagui Dekel-Chen, are expected to be released as part of the first phase of the Israel-Hamas ceasefire deal that emerged Wednesday after being held for 15-months by Hamas since the start of the war in Gaza, according to a senior administration official.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation
